title: Availability Measurement for GitLab SaaS Services
headings: Availability Measurement for GitLab SaaS Services
GitLab Service Level Agreement - Availability Definition
text:
Availability Measurement for GitLab SaaS Services

This is a Controlled Document

GitLab Service Level Agreement - Availability Definition

The following service level agreement applies to SaaS Software purchased or renewed as Ultimate tier on or after 2026-01-01.

Service Availability Commitment

GitLab commits to make the Covered Experiences available with a Monthly Uptime Percentage of at least 99.9% during each calendar month.

The following GitLab features and services are covered under this SLA (“Covered Experiences”):

Issues and Merge Requests

Git Operations (push, pull, clone via HTTPS and SSH)

Container Registry operations

Package Registry operations

API Requests (limited to the above Covered Experiences only)

Downtime Minute Definition

A “Downtime Minute” occurs when:

The Service experiences degraded availability, meaning 5% or more of Valid Customer Requests to Covered Experiences in a given minute result in server errors
(defined as HTTP 5xx status codes or connection timeouts exceeding 30 seconds)
as determined by GitLab’s internal and external monitoring systems.

Downtime Minutes do not include any Excluded Minutes as defined in this document.

Valid Request Definition

“Valid Request” means a request that meets all of the following criteria:

Properly authenticated and authorized - The request includes valid authentication
credentials and the user has appropriate permissions for the requested operation

clude any Excluded Minutes as defined in this document.

Valid Request Definition

“Valid Request” means a request that meets all of the following criteria:

Properly authenticated and authorized - The request includes valid authentication
credentials and the user has appropriate permissions for the requested operation

Properly authenticated and authorized - The request includes valid authentication
credentials and the user has appropriate permissions for the requested operation

Correctly formed - The request: Uses documented API endpoints, parameters, and methods Follows GitLab’s published API specifications and documentation Contains syntactically correct Git commands (for Git operations) Includes required headers and properly formatted request bodies

Correctly formed - The request:

Uses documented API endpoints, parameters, and methods

Follows GitLab’s published API specifications and documentation

Contains syntactically correct Git commands (for Git operations)

Includes required headers and properly formatted request bodies

Within usage limits - The request: Does not exceed documented rate limits Does not exceed size limits (e.g., file size, repository size, payload size) Originates from non-blocked IP addresses or users Complies with fair use policies

Within usage limits - The request:

Does not exceed documented rate limits

Does not exceed size limits (e.g., file size, repository size, payload size)

Originates from non-blocked IP addresses or users

Complies with fair use policies

Originates from non-blocked IP addresses or users Complies with fair use policies

Within usage limits - The request:

Does not exceed documented rate limits

Does not exceed size limits (e.g., file size, repository size, payload size)

Originates from non-blocked IP addresses or users

Complies with fair use policies

Non-malicious - The request: Does not attempt to exploit security vulnerabilities Does not contain malicious code or payloads Is not part of a DDoS or other attack pattern Does not attempt to circumvent access controls

Non-malicious - The request:

Does not attempt to exploit security vulnerabilities

Does not contain malicious code or payloads

Is not part of a DDoS or other attack pattern

Does not attempt to circumvent access controls

Supported operations - The request: Targets features included in the customer’s subscription tier Uses generally available features (not experimental/beta)

Supported operations - The request:

Targets features included in the customer’s subscription tier

Uses generally available features (not experimental/beta)

The following are excluded from Valid Requests:

Requests that receive HTTP 4xx errors due to client-side issues

Requests to deprecated API endpoints after the deprecation date

Requests from automated testing or monitoring tools not operated by GitLab

Preflight/OPTIONS requests

Health check or status endpoint requests

The following minutes are excluded from being counted as Downtime Minutes:

Scheduled Maintenance windows

Emergency maintenance necessary to address critical security or data integrity issues

GitLab Service Level Agreement - Availability Definition
text:
mated testing or monitoring tools not operated by GitLab

Preflight/OPTIONS requests

Health check or status endpoint requests

The following minutes are excluded from being counted as Downtime Minutes:

Scheduled Maintenance windows

Emergency maintenance necessary to address critical security or data integrity issues

Force Majeure events or general Internet connectivity issues

Issues caused by Customer’s use of the Service in violation of the Terms of Service

Issues caused by equipment, software, or services not provided or controlled by GitLab

Features or services explicitly labeled as Alpha, Beta, or Preview

Calculating Monthly Uptime Percentage

“Monthly Uptime Percentage” is calculated as:

“Total Minutes in Month” = Total number of minutes in the calendar month

“Downtime Minutes” = Total number of minutes in a calendar month when
Covered Experiences met the Downtime Minute definition,
excluding any Excluded Minutes

If the Monthly Uptime Percentage falls below 99.9%, GitLab Ultimate tier Customers may request (defined below) Service Credits according to:

Monthly Uptime Percentage

Credit Request Process

To receive a Service Credit, an eligible customer must:

Submit a Support request at support.gitlab.com within thirty (30) days after the end of the affected month;

Include all relevant information about when the SaaS Service was unavailable; and

Provide reasonable detail about the attempted access to Covered Experiences

eceive a Service Credit, an eligible customer must:

Submit a Support request at support.gitlab.com within thirty (30) days after the end of the affected month;

Include all relevant information about when the SaaS Service was unavailable; and

Provide reasonable detail about the attempted access to Covered Experiences

Once received, GitLab will then verify the claim against its own internal monitoring data and, if validated, issue Service Credits against Customer’s next issued invoice. In no event shall an issued Service Credit result in a refund of Fees to Customer.

Raise a request through support.GitLab.com to check if you are eligible for credits caused by downtime.
